codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses the prior m7 failure: RunService provider-only failover alternates now come from scoreAndRankRoutes()-derived ordering, and architecture wording now matches weighted composite scorecard behavior. The original blocker from m7-runtime-router-wiring-to-scorecard is resolved.

issues.description: The new RunService VAL-EXEC-008 test validates successful failover and single usage record, but it does not assert attempted route slot order directly; BurnBarFailoverSimulatorProviderExecutor fails/succeeds by call count only and ignores route identity, so this test cannot independently distinguish scorecard ordering from legacy ordering.

sharedStateObservations.observation: runtime-orchestration-worker SKILL validation command snippets still reference legacy OpenBurnBar* test filters that do not match the current BurnBar* command canon in services.yaml.

summary: Reviewed fix transcript skeleton plus both feature diffs (original b3e48dd9 and fix 4dd11f9a). The prior blocking issue is fixed: executeProviderOnlyRun now sources failover alternates from scoreAndRankRoutes() instead of legacy candidateRoutes() ordering, and architecture documentation is aligned to weighted composite routing semantics.
